channel.title: Platforms on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Platforms on emsenn.net

item.title: Syntactic branding
item.description:
Syntactic branding

Syntactic branding is emsenn’s term, introduced in “Marked speech, borrowed grammar” (2025-04-05), for a style of speaking that aligns the speaker with a specific affective register and demographic code. The speaker adopts grammatical forms — often drawn from appropriated linguistic traditions and flattened into repeatable formats — that signal membership in a recognizable cultural position.

What distinguishes syntactic branding from ordinary stylistic variation is the role of platform infrastructure in reinforcing it. Algorithmic feedback loops reward certain phrase structures with visibility: posts that use phrases like “it’s giving” or “go off king” generate engagement, and that engagement produces more visibility, which in turn encourages more users to adopt the same structures. The grammar becomes optimized for circulation rather than for communication. It functions as a user interface component — a way of interacting with the platform’s reward mechanisms — rather than as a way of speaking to another person.

item.title: Accepting the Web as a Platform, not a Place
item.description:
• History
    • first the internet
    • then web
    • commerce
    • platforms
  • internal mental image of web presence didn’t change from the internet days
    • personal website
    • platforms point back there
  • practiced denial of change
    • out-of-step with the theory
    • out-of-step with normal practices
      • even “The Internet” has become a platform (IndieWeb, ActivityPub)
    • out-of-step with my own practices
  • compare to living on a land in a territory, to living as a subject in an environment
    • different values, different modes of living
    • personal websites as living on a land
    • platform presence as subject in environment
    • different modes of governance
  • self-governing as required for both
    • but different governance technologies apply:
      • personal websites
        • permanence, backlinks, “sediment”, foundation, structure
      • platform
        • fungibility, presence, ???
    • practices of self-governance for platformized web life?
